content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>有同事提供 "G:\AI\Prompt的建議.txt" 的建議，你覺得有可以吸收和強化bridge的prompt的地方嗎?</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-07-07</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
我已經把 bridge 的 prompt 組成與處理流程完整看過一遍。以下是整理：

## Bridge prompt 的組成與處理流程

整體是「**session 建立時凍結一份 preamble** + **每輪動態 enrichment** + **回覆端 token 抽取**」三段式。

### 1. Session 建立時：Preamble 一次組好並凍結（`sessionManager.ts` `create()`）

`SessionManager.create()` 在 chat 第一次建 session 時組出 `memoryPreamble`，之後整個 session 不再重建（對應 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`）：

- **`buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`**（`memory.ts:261`）依序串接：
  1. **Tool notes**（~14 個 token 協定說明）：SEND_FILE、ASK、SCHEDULE、SKILL_USED、GOAL_DONE、RESTART、STICKER、WIKI_QUERY、SKILL_PROPOSE、CONTINUE，加上條件式的 RELAY_DELEGATE（讀 `relay-peers.json`）、SPECIALIST_PROXY / PARALLEL_DELEGATE（讀 `specialists.json`）、RTK shell 提示
  2. **Environment block**：MEMORY_DIR / SKILL_DIR / AGENT_CONFIG_DIR / USER_ID（讓 SKILL.md 跨機器可移植），外加 AI.md 分層載入規則和 CodeGraph 使用規則
  3. **User profile**（`user-profile.md`）
  4. **Memory block**（`buildMemoryBlock`）：最近 N 條 facts（`memoryPreambleTail`，可經 local LLM 壓縮）分成 user-preference / agent-notes 兩軌 + 舊 facts 的 topic shard 索引（叫 agent 用 `list_facts` MCP tool 撈）+ 壓縮版七項 Agent disciplines
  5. **Wiki index**（`wiki/index.md` 的頁面標題清單）、**Document indexes**（`indexes/` 樹狀索引 + pending ingests）、**Scripts block**（`scripts/tool-*` 自建工具清單）
- create() 再往後接 **一次性恢復區塊**：working-state（讀完即刪）、session archive 還原（上個 session 的摘要，trivial session 會 carry-forward）、dream High Priority 建議（48h 新鮮度閘門）、進行中 relay task 清單
- 每段長度記進 `preambleBreakdown` 供 `/context` UI 顯示 token 去向

**Provider 差異**：ACP（Kiro/Codex/Claude adapter）沒有 system prompt 通道，所以 `preambleInjected=false`，preamble 在**第一個 user prompt 前綴注入**（`run-prompt.ts:380`）；Direct provider（API 直連）直接當 system message，`preambleInjected=true`。Local（Ollama）走 `buildLocalPreamble` 精簡版（<2k tokens）。

### 2. 每輪送出前：Enrichment pipeline（`index.ts:1029-1155`）

使用者訊息在進 `runPrompt` 前會被層層前綴（local provider 全跳過）：

1. **NotebookLM embedding routing** — 語意相近的 notebook 提示
2. **Wiki retrieval** — 相關 wiki 頁全文注入（per-session 用 `injectedWikiSlugs` 去重）
3. **Memory recall** — topic shard 的語意檢索 facts（用 `tailFacts` 排除 preamble 已含的、`wikiHitSlugs` 排除 wiki 已覆蓋的主題）
4. **Skill routing** — 相關 skill 提示（`injectedSkills` 去重）
5. **Intent classification** — 像 /command 意圖時提示改用指令
6. **Specialist routing hint** — 建議走 SPECIALIST_PROXY
7. **Negation reflexion hint** — 使用者說「不對/重做」時加自省提示

然後 `runPrompt`（`run-prompt.ts:304`）再加：**70% context budget warning**（一次性）、**preamble**（首輪）、goal 模式下高風險決策的 **self-fusion 反方觀點**，最後 `session.client.prompt(promptText)` 送出。

### 3. 回覆端處理

- Streaming：`applyUpdate` 累積 `buffer`/`thinkingBuffer`/`toolLog`，節流 edit Telegram placeholder；無 in-flight turn 的輸出走 orphan-turn 緩衝 debounce flush
- Turn 結束：新 token 統一由 `observerTransformer.transform` 抽取，三個老 token（SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/ASK）的 extract helper 留在 `index.ts`；RELAY_DELEGATE 有 streaming 期間的早期捕捉（`captureRelayDelegateTokensFromText`），因為 Kiro CLI 可能把 token 從最終文字剝掉
- 連續 2 次 tool 失敗會往 `toolLog` 注入 Reflexion hint；context 過大時 agent 可發 `<<RESTART>>`，restart 前把 working state 存進 facts，下個 session 由 create() 的一次性區塊接回

一句話總結：**preamble 是 session 級的凍結快照（身份/工具協定/記憶索引），每輪 prompt 是「動態檢索注入 + 使用者訊息」，回覆是「streaming 累積 + token 協定抽取」**。有想深入哪一段（例如 observerTransformer 的 token 抽取細節或 provider 抽象）再說。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>有同事提供 "G:\AI\Prompt的建議.txt" 的建議，你覺得有可以吸收和強化bridge的prompt的地方嗎?</user_request>

```xml
<observation>

  <!--

      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
```

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-07-07T07:25:54.554Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\AI\telegram-kiro-bridge-main</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\Prompt的建議.txt\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"G:\\\\AI\\\\Prompt的建議.txt\",\"content\":\"​-任務\\n​\\n這是我這輩子唯一一次使用 Fable 5 的機會。\\n​\\n這個 session 結束後，我的環境將由較小的模型（Sonnet、Opus、Haiku 等）長期運作。\\n​\\n所以你的任務只有一個：\\n​\\n把你的判斷力轉成可長期沿用的制度與檔案，讓之後每一個較弱模型的 session 都因此變強。用這個 session 立制度，不要拿去執行日常任務。\\n​\\n你的讀者是弱模型——規則要具體、可執行、有判準與範例，抽象要求等於沒寫；所有產出須在 Sonnet 等級跑得動。\\n​\\n注意：某些請求會被安全機制自動導向到 Opus 4.8，你未必總會察覺，所以別把價值綁在特定型號上，要綁在「把高階判斷轉成便宜模型也能長期沿用的制度」上。\\n​\\nFable 5 的額度是稀缺的，當預算來管理。\\n​\\n​\\n-作業規則\\n​\\n1. 先盤點環境再產出。查 CLAUDE.md、可用 subagent、當前實際可用的模型與 effort 參數、MCP、skill、記憶機制。盤點完先回報「查到什麼／假設什麼／查不到什麼」，再開始產出。開場問題最多五題，之後自主作業不再停等。\\n​\\n2. 指揮官不下場，以省額度。\\n​\\n大量讀取、掃 repo、查網頁、批次改檔、驗證一律派便宜 subagent，主對話只進結論。\\n​\\n高階主線只花在「換便宜模型就掉品質」的判斷上。\\n​\\n3. 價值排序、隨做隨寫。每完成一項立刻存檔再做下一項；隨時可能中斷，存檔的就是全部。\\n​\\n4. 改既有檔前先備份。新內容寫新檔；\\n​\\nCLAUDE.md 只放索引路由，不塞長內容。\\n​\\n5. 型號與參數先查證，絕不憑記憶填。\\n​\\n查得到寫實際值，查不到寫「待使用者填 寫」並在回報中標出。\\n​\\n6. 核心常載檔有上限：CLAUDE.md 本體 ≤150 行且只當索引；\\n​\\n其直接引用的常載檔合計 ≤500 行；其餘做成按需引用檔。超過就精簡或拆分。\\n​\\n7. 若時間窗口允許，可跨 2–3 個 session 完成，每段之間派 fresh-context subagent 驗證再續，不賭不能中斷的長 session。\\n​\\n​\\n-交付清單（按價值排序）\\n​\\nA. 快速診斷（先寫，供後面引用）：\\n​\\n此 harness 最漏 token、最易失焦、最易出錯的前三名，各附一個弱模型能照做的具體修法。\\n​\\n​\\nB. 重寫 CLAUDE.md：\\n​\\n收斂重複、刪過時、長內容抽離成引用檔，套「弱模型明確、強模型留白」，守規則 6 的上限。\\n​​\\nC. 模型調度守則（獨立檔）：\\n​\\n指揮官不下場；\\n​\\n任務交辦三要素（目標與動機、驗收條件、回報格式）；\\n​\\n顯式指定 model 與 effort（依規則 5 查證後的實際型號）；\\n​\\n回報合約（subagent 只回結論與「檔案:行號」，長產物存檔後傳路徑）；\\n​\\n升降級路徑（小模型錯一次升級、中階同一子任務連錯兩次帶完整失敗軌跡升級、解出模式降回便宜模型批次套用、同一件事最多重試兩輪）；\\n​\\n驗證不自驗（派 fresh-context agent：檔案用 read-back、程式碼用測試或實跑、高風險判斷加第二意見或多答案評審擇優）。 \\n​​\\nD. 判斷力外化（獨立檔）：\\n​\\n把高階判斷寫成弱模型可執行的 rubric 與 checklist，每條附一正例一反例。\\n​\\n至少涵蓋：\\n​\\n何時升級模型、何時算真的完成、何時停下來問使用者、什麼訊號代表方向錯該換路而非重試、品質底線怎麼驗。 \\n​​\\nE. 任務交辦 prompt 範本：\\n​\\n含驗收條件與回報格式的填空，任務型態各一份——搜尋、實作、重構、研究、審查。 F. 維護協議：哪些檔弱模型可自行改、哪些動前先問使用者、踩雷教訓寫回哪個檔與格式、累積多長要精簡。 \\n​\\n​\\nG. 給未來 session 的信：\\n​\\n三件我沒問但你認為最重要的事；這套制度最可能的退化方式與預防法；誠實列出你這次哪幾份產出信心最低、為什麼。\\n​​\\n-收尾（必做）\\n​\\n1. 派 fresh-context subagent 對抗審查全部產出：規則互相打架、路徑或工具名錯誤、型號是否為查證後的實際值、弱模型會誤讀的模糊句。\\n​\\n修完為止。\\n​​\\n2. read-back 驗證每個檔確實建立、內容完整。\\n​​\\n3. 給我一頁總結：\\n​改了什麼、為什麼、明天怎麼開始用。\\n​​\\n4. context 或額度快用完：\\n​立即停止產出，先完成收尾 1–3，未完成項寫進 G 交接。\\n​\\n​\\n-誠實條款\\n​\\n拆解、驗證、多樣本評審補得了執行品質；\\n​模糊題與品味判斷補不了，遇到就寫明處理方式（升級模型、外部第二意見、或明說做不到）。\\n​不確定就查，查不到標註不編造——特別是當前可用的型號與參數、以及「被導向到 Opus 4.8 的請求是否消耗此窗口額度」這類事，查不到就寫「未確認，建議到 usage 儀表板實測」。\\n\",\"numLines\":107,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":107}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

message.content:
PROGRESS SUMMARY CHECKPOINT
===========================
Write progress notes of what was done, what was learned, and what's next. This is a checkpoint to capture progress so far. The session is ongoing - you may receive more requests and tool executions after this summary. Write "next_steps" as the current trajectory of work (what's actively being worked on or coming up next), not as post-session future work. Always write at least a minimal summary explaining current progress, even if work is still in early stages, so that users see a summary output tied to each request.

Claude's Full Response to User:
我已經把 bridge 的 prompt 組成與處理流程完整看過一遍。以下是整理：

## Bridge prompt 的組成與處理流程

整體是「**session 建立時凍結一份 preamble** + **每輪動態 enrichment** + **回覆端 token 抽取**」三段式。

### 1. Session 建立時：Preamble 一次組好並凍結（`sessionManager.ts` `create()`）

`SessionManager.create()` 在 chat 第一次建 session 時組出 `memoryPreamble`，之後整個 session 不再重建（對應 `POLICIES/preamble-frozen-snapshot.md`）：

- **`buildPreambleWithBreakdown()`**（`memory.ts:261`）依序串接：
  1. **Tool notes**（~14 個 token 協定說明）：SEND_FILE、ASK、SCHEDULE、SKILL_USED、GOAL_DONE、RESTART、STICKER、WIKI_QUERY、SKILL_PROPOSE、CONTINUE，加上條件式的 RELAY_DELEGATE（讀 `relay-peers.json`）、SPECIALIST_PROXY / PARALLEL_DELEGATE（讀 `specialists.json`）、RTK shell 提示
  2. **Environment block**：MEMORY_DIR / SKILL_DIR / AGENT_CONFIG_DIR / USER_ID（讓 SKILL.md 跨機器可移植），外加 AI.md 分層載入規則和 CodeGraph 使用規則
  3. **User profile**（`user-profile.md`）
  4. **Memory block**（`buildMemoryBlock`）：最近 N 條 facts（`memoryPreambleTail`，可經 local LLM 壓縮）分成 user-preference / agent-notes 兩軌 + 舊 facts 的 topic shard 索引（叫 agent 用 `list_facts` MCP tool 撈）+ 壓縮版七項 Agent disciplines
  5. **Wiki index**（`wiki/index.md` 的頁面標題清單）、**Document indexes**（`indexes/` 樹狀索引 + pending ingests）、**Scripts block**（`scripts/tool-*` 自建工具清單）
- create() 再往後接 **一次性恢復區塊**：working-state（讀完即刪）、session archive 還原（上個 session 的摘要，trivial session 會 carry-forward）、dream High Priority 建議（48h 新鮮度閘門）、進行中 relay task 清單
- 每段長度記進 `preambleBreakdown` 供 `/context` UI 顯示 token 去向

**Provider 差異**：ACP（Kiro/Codex/Claude adapter）沒有 system prompt 通道，所以 `preambleInjected=false`，preamble 在**第一個 user prompt 前綴注入**（`run-prompt.ts:380`）；Direct provider（API 直連）直接當 system message，`preambleInjected=true`。Local（Ollama）走 `buildLocalPreamble` 精簡版（<2k tokens）。

### 2. 每輪送出前：Enrichment pipeline（`index.ts:1029-1155`）

使用者訊息在進 `runPrompt` 前會被層層前綴（local provider 全跳過）：

1. **NotebookLM embedding routing** — 語意相近的 notebook 提示
2. **Wiki retrieval** — 相關 wiki 頁全文注入（per-session 用 `injectedWikiSlugs` 去重）
3. **Memory recall** — topic shard 的語意檢索 facts（用 `tailFacts` 排除 preamble 已含的、`wikiHitSlugs` 排除 wiki 已覆蓋的主題）
4. **Skill routing** — 相關 skill 提示（`injectedSkills` 去重）
5. **Intent classification** — 像 /command 意圖時提示改用指令
6. **Specialist routing hint** — 建議走 SPECIALIST_PROXY
7. **Negation reflexion hint** — 使用者說「不對/重做」時加自省提示

然後 `runPrompt`（`run-prompt.ts:304`）再加：**70% context budget warning**（一次性）、**preamble**（首輪）、goal 模式下高風險決策的 **self-fusion 反方觀點**，最後 `session.client.prompt(promptText)` 送出。

### 3. 回覆端處理

- Streaming：`applyUpdate` 累積 `buffer`/`thinkingBuffer`/`toolLog`，節流 edit Telegram placeholder；無 in-flight turn 的輸出走 orphan-turn 緩衝 debounce flush
- Turn 結束：新 token 統一由 `observerTransformer.transform` 抽取，三個老 token（SEND_FILE/SCHEDULE/ASK）的 extract helper 留在 `index.ts`；RELAY_DELEGATE 有 streaming 期間的早期捕捉（`captureRelayDelegateTokensFromText`），因為 Kiro CLI 可能把 token 從最終文字剝掉
- 連續 2 次 tool 失敗會往 `toolLog` 注入 Reflexion hint；context 過大時 agent 可發 `<<RESTART>>`，restart 前把 working state 存進 facts，下個 session 由 create() 的一次性區塊接回

一句話總結：**preamble 是 session 級的凍結快照（身份/工具協定/記憶索引），每輪 prompt 是「動態檢索注入 + 使用者訊息」，回覆是「streaming 累積 + token 協定抽取」**。有想深入哪一段（例如 observerTransformer 的 token 抽取細節或 provider 抽象）再說。

Respond in this XML format:
<summary>
  <request>[Short title capturing the user's request AND the substance of what was discussed/done]</request>
  <investigated>[What has been explored so far? What was examined?]</investigated>
  <learned>[What have you learned about how things work?]</learned>
  <completed>[What work has been completed so far? What has shipped or changed?]</completed>
  <next_steps>[What are you actively working on or planning to work on next in this session?]</next_steps>
  <notes>[Additional insights or observations about the current progress]</notes>
</summary>

IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this next PROGRESS SUMMARY - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the summary content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful summary content.

Thank you, this summary will be very useful for keeping track of our progress!

content.thinking:
The primary session just read a file called "Prompt的建議.txt" (Prompt Suggestions). This is a detailed document in Chinese that contains suggestions for how to work with Fable 5 (a Claude model) to create lasting institutional knowledge and workflows that will work with weaker models (Sonnet, Opus, Haiku) in the future.

The document is quite comprehensive and includes:
- A task statement about using Fable 5 as a one-time opportunity to create systems/files that weaker models can use long-term
- Operating rules (inventory environment first, commander doesn't execute, value ordering, backup before changes, verify model parameters, etc.)
- A deliverables list (quick diagnostics, rewrite CLAUDE.md, model scheduling rules, externalize judgment, task prompt templates, maintenance protocols, letter to future sessions)
- Closing procedures (adversarial review, read-back verification, summary)
- Honesty clause (don't fabricate, mark uncertainties)

This is a discovery/learning phase where the user is asking if there are things from this suggestion document that could be absorbed or strengthen the "bridge" prompt.

The Claude response in the primary session provided a detailed breakdown of how the bridge prompt system works, explaining:
1. Session creation with frozen preamble
2. Per-turn enrichment pipeline
3. Response processing with token extraction

So the primary session is currently in a phase of:
- Understanding the colleague's suggestions
- Comparing them to the current bridge prompt architecture
- Potentially looking to improve the bridge prompt system based on these suggestions

Since this is early in the work - just a file read and an explanation - I should create an observation about this discovery/learning phase.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Bridge prompt architecture mapped against Fable 5 optimization suggestions</title>
  <subtitle>Colleague provided guidance document for creating durable prompt systems that work across model tiers</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Bridge uses three-stage prompt architecture: frozen session preamble, dynamic per-turn enrichment, and response token extraction</fact>
    <fact>Preamble is assembled once at session creation by SessionManager.create() and includes tool protocols, environment, user profile, memory blocks, and wiki/document indexes</fact>
    <fact>Per-turn enrichment pipeline injects NotebookLM embeddings, wiki retrieval, memory recall, skill routing, and intent classification before sending prompts</fact>
    <fact>Colleague's suggestion document emphasizes commander-doesn't-execute pattern, explicit model/effort parameters, verification by fresh-context agents, and externalizing judgment into rubrics</fact>
    <fact>Suggestion document prioritizes creating lasting institutional knowledge files that weaker models can execute, with CLAUDE.md ≤150 lines and referenced files ≤500 lines total</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The primary session received a colleague's comprehensive guidance document (G:\AI\Prompt的建議.txt) for optimizing work with Fable 5, focusing on creating durable prompt systems and workflows that remain effective when executed by weaker models (Sonnet, Opus, Haiku). The document emphasizes several key principles: (1) commanders should delegate execution to cheaper subagents rather than executing directly, (2) all model types and effort parameters must be verified not assumed, (3) judgment should be externalized into rubrics and checklists that weaker models can follow, (4) verification should use fresh-context agents rather than self-verification, and (5) core documentation should be concise with size limits enforced.

The primary session then mapped out the bridge's existing three-stage prompt architecture: SessionManager.create() freezes a preamble containing tool protocols, environment variables, memory indexes, and user profile at session start; each turn then runs an enrichment pipeline that dynamically injects relevant wiki pages, memory facts, skills, and routing hints; finally responses are processed through streaming buffers and token extraction via observerTransformer. The bridge's current design already implements some suggested patterns (frozen preambles, memory indexing, skill routing) but may benefit from adopting the colleague's emphasis on explicit model scheduling, verification contracts, and judgment externalization.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>trade-off</concept>
  </concepts>
